creator: Alexander@Leidinger.net
text:
Quoting the website:
	K-3D is the free (as in freedom) 3D modeling, animation, and rendering
	system. Despite the name it didn't uses any KDE library, it uses GTK+.


	On -CURRENT I get a segfault if I try to run it, but it does this
	in libc after calling popen(), so it may be my environment (the
	author says it works on his linux box, and the call to popen() looks
	right).
	A workaround for the segfault is to remove the shader path variable
	in the k3d start script, but in this case you didn't have shaders.
	I haven't tested it on RELENG_4.

	An enhancement would be to depend on linux-bmrt and to remove the
	"--without-bmrt" in the Makefile of the port, but I had no time to
	test it and I didn't think I will have time soon to test it.
